judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports cover grouping, ordering, and the F-06 escalation equally well, but Report 2 additionally nails the shared RESULTS_DB collision risk and mitigation (expectation d) that Report 1 only mentions in passing, making Report 2 the stronger plan overall.

report_1.evidence: Same-file contention on `pipeline/registry.py`. F-02, F-05, F-09 all edit constants in this file — trivial individually, but three parallel patches to a 3-line file conflict; give it to one owner.

report_2.evidence: Batch A — Config (`registry.py`): F-02, F-05, F-09.** All three edit the same file (`MODEL_ENDPOINTS`, `DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION`, `DEFAULT_DATASET`); one owner does them in series.

report_1.evidence: Because owners hold disjoint files, the four fix-streams (A/B/C/D) run fully in parallel ... B does F-02/F-05/F-09 ... C writes F-11 only after F-01.

report_2.evidence: Wave 1 — four fully-parallel batches, one owner each (files are disjoint, so no collisions): ... Batch A — Config (`registry.py`): F-02, F-05, F-09.** All three edit the same file ... one owner does them in series. ... Batch C — Rubric (`rubric.py`): F-01, paired with F-11.

report_1.evidence: F-06 — a scoring-policy change, not a mechanical fix. "Tighten `PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS`..." gives no target values. Whatever numbers an engineer picks will shift every borderline score in production, so it needs an owner/product decision on the target thresholds (and a test pinning them) before it's coded.

report_2.evidence: F-06 — do not hand out as a blind fix; escalate for a decision.** "Tighten the thresholds so borderline answers score lower" is a **user-facing scoring-behavior/quality-policy change**, not a mechanical bug, and it specifies **no target values**. ... This needs an owner of eval quality to pick new thresholds and validate them

Shared test-resource collision.** `tests/conftest.py:4` defaults `RESULTS_DB` to a single path `/var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db`. Multiple engineers running `pytest` on the same machine hit the *same* DB, cross-contaminating parallel runs. Mitigation ... each worktree exports its own `RESULTS_DB`.
